title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During the Halloween party organized by Royana Drecker, a mirror ritual involving a light beam reveals a figure with a large beard. Beatrice compares the figure to the rock star Eddie Prescott, but Drecker explains that Nick and his friend Desmond, an experienced photographer, staged the effect using costumes and lighting for the guests' entertainment. Following a dinner of snacks and cakes, the festivities conclude with a "dragon hunt" featuring a dish of burning grapes served on a green tablecloth. As the party ends, Judith Butler notes the need to clean up the resulting mess.

In a London apartment, Hercule Poirot is disappointed when his butler, George, informs him that Solomon Levy is unable to visit to debate the Cunningham Road Public Baths murder case due to a severe cold. Poirot soon receives a telephone call from his friend, Ariadne Oliver, who sounds breathless and highly agitated. Recognizing that Mrs. Oliver's visits often involve long sessions of venting grievances, Poirot invites her over and prepares lemon barley tea and white wine for himself.

Mrs. Oliver arrives at the apartment in a state of extreme distress, wearing a soaked fisherman's rain hat and an oilcloth raincoat. She repeatedly expresses a sudden, intense hatred and fear of water, claiming she had never previously considered what water could do. Poirot observes her agitation with interest as George assists her in removing her wet clothing.
